evals.prompt: Grade this /goal before I run it: /goal improve the app until it is better
evals.expected_output: Returns a low grade, identifies missing measurable done criteria and context, and provides an optimized /goal rewrite.
assertions: Includes a letter grade and numeric score.
Flags vague scope/context as a blocker or high-impact fix.
Provides an optimized /goal rewrite with validation and done conditions.
expectations: Includes a letter grade and numeric score.

evals.prompt: Use goal grader on this: /goal Delete old production users and deploy immediately.
evals.expected_output: Treats destructive and deployment actions as blockers without explicit approval gates.
assertions: Flags deployment or external side effects without approval.
Does not mark the goal ready to run.
Adds explicit approval gates in the optimized rewrite.
expectations: Flags deployment or external side effects without approval.
Does not mark the goal ready to run.

evals.prompt: Score this Codex goal and improve it: /goal Complete checkout refactor in repo without changing payment provider. First read docs/checkout.md. Work in checkpoints, run npm test after each change, and done when checkout tests pass and a report is written. Pause before deploying or changing credentials.
evals.expected_output: Returns a good grade with minor improvements, preserves the single objective, and reinforces completion/blocking rules.
assertions: Rewards clear objective, context, validation, and safety gates.
Keeps the rewrite as one executable goal.
Mentions completion criteria and stop/block behavior.
Does not invent extra unrelated objectives.
